BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Sanctuary Centers of Santa Barbara Inc

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: We provide comprehensive mental healthcare that transforms lives.
IRS990.MissionDesc: We provide comprehensive mental healthcare that transforms lives.
IRS990.Desc: Arlington Day Treatment Center: the purpose of the Arlington Day Treatment Center is to provide support, education, treatment, and a solid foundation from which clients can gain and maintain stability in their lives. The Arlington program consists of a variety of therapy groups/process groups, some using evocative materials, as well as psycho-educational, socialization and therapeutic recreational groups. During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, 190 people were treated at the Arlington facility.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: Enhanced Care Management: The ECM program aims to address the clinical and nonclinical needs of high cost and high need MediCal (CenCal) recipients through community-based, person centered care. Operating out of the recently reopened Integrated Health Clinic, which also helps members access affordable health care in the Santa Barbara community. During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, 217 people were treated by the ECM Program.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: Sanctuary House: for adults who do not need (or prefer an alternative to) psychiatric hospitalization but who are unable to live independently. Sanctuary House provides attractive surroundings with an ideal combination of privacy and family atmosphere. The Sanctuary House program services a maximum of 12 adults who are experiencing emotional difficulties with or without substance abuse issues. The program focuses on teaching clients independent living skills and socialization skills necessary for successful community living. In addition, Sanctuary House provides a comprehensive clinical program designed to help clients cope with intra-psychic issues that interfere with an independent lifestyle. During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, 21 people were treated at the Sanctuary House facility.

OtherAssetsOrgGrp.Desc: Loan fees, net of amortization expense

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Organization is a voluntary health and welfare organization that is exempt from taxes on income under Internal Revenue Code section 501(c)(3) and California Revenue and Taxation Code 23701(d). The IRS classified the organization as one that is not a private foundation within the meaning of section 509(a) of the Code because it is an organization described in section(s) 509(a)(1) and 170(b)(1)(A)(vi).The Organization has adopted Financial Accounting Standards Board Accounting Standards Codification (ASC) Section 740-10, which clarifies the accounting for uncertainty in income taxes. ASC Section 740-10 prescribes a recognition threshold and measurement attribute for the financial statement recognition and measurement of a tax position taken or expected to be taken in a tax return. ASC Section 740-10 requires that an organization recognize in the financial statements the impact of the tax position if that position will more likely than not be sustained on audit, based on the technical merits of the position. As of and for the year ended June 30, 2024, the Organization had no material unrecognized tax benefits, tax penalties or interest. The Organization's Forms 990, Return of Organization Exempt from Income Tax, for each of the tax years ended June 30; 2023, 2022, and 2021, are subject to examination by the IRS, generally for 3 years after they were filed.The Organizations Forms 199, California Exempt Organization Return, for each of the tax years ended 2023, 2022, 2021 and 2020 are subject to examination by the Franchise Tax Board, generally for 4 years after they were filed.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Part XI, Line 2d: Other revenue amounts included in F/S but not included on form 990
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Book loss on disposal of fixed assets $-108815 Intercompany Rental Income $332100

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Schedule L, Part V Supplemental Information
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A current Director of the Organizations Board of Directors is an attorney with a law firm that performs legal work for the Organization. The Organization paid the firm approximately $627 during the year ended June 30, 2024, for services primarily relating to work with a donors legal counsel in obtaining court approval for a large charitable donation for the new building project. The Board of Directors of the Organization approved the services and fees in accordance with its conflict of interest policies.

OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES 4: COD Outpatient Treatment Program: the Co-Occurring Disorders (COD) Outpatient Treatment program operates in conjunction with all of our other programs, and provides specialized counseling services tailored to the needs of people diagnosed with both mental health and substance use disorders. This program provides counseling, education, and practical guidelines for coping with both diagnoses. Other services include community referral services and assistance with community re-integration. During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, 233 people were treated by the COD Department.Total program expenses were $717,713. Total revenue was $641,783. OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES 5: Hollister Apartments: the Hollister Apartments offer a supported independent living program through the provision of affordable studio units and supportive services for low income persons living with mental illness. As such, a continuum of services is provided at the housing site. As of and for the year ended June 30, 2024, there were eight people residing in the Hollister Apartments. Total program expenses were $271,848. Total program revenue was $168,180. OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES 6: Chartman: Chartman is an electronic patient record (EHR) software system that staff use for efficient charting, HIPAA compliance, instant access for charting and billing, and all patient/client record keeping. Total program expenses were $56,202. OTHER PROGRAM SERVICES 7: Enhanced Care Management: The ECM program aims to address the clinical and nonclinical needs of high cost and high need MediCal (CenCal) recipients through community-based, person centered care. Operating out of the recently reopened Integrated Health Clinic, which also helps members access affordable health care in the Santa Barbara community. During the fiscal year ended June 30, 2024, 217 people were treated by the ECM Program.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Management reviews and approves the 990 for filing and advises the Board and Finance Committee of its review. Copies of the 990 will be made available to any Board and Finance Committee member upon request.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The conflict of interest policy is distributed annually to all Board Members, Committee Members, and key employees. Each recipient returns a signed and dated response form indicating they have reviewed the policy, noting any conflicts that exist.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The determination of compensation of the Executive Director of the organization involves a review between the Chairman of the Board and/or Executive Committee using compensation comparability data at time of raises.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The determination of compensation of the Officers of the organization involves a review between the President/CEO and the Chairman of the Board and/or Executive Committee using compensation comparability data at time of raises.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization makes its governing documents available by mail upon request.
Book loss on disposal of fixed assets = -$108815
The Organization's Board of Directors assumes responsibility for oversight of the audit and selection of an independent accountant. No changes since the prior year.
